headline: Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
description: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

headline: Hantavirus: Many unknowns surround an 'unprecedented and worrying' outbreak
French presidential election: Proposals abound, but who will champion them?
Sarkozy spared electronic ankle tag in re-election campaign financing case
description: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy will not serve his sentence with an electronic ankle tag, with a source citing his age in the decision.

headline: Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
description: Police forces under Hamas's authority are regularly targeted by the Israeli army. Israel describes these as preventive strikes, which target officers who also serve in the Islamist movement's armed wing. Civil society actors, on the other hand, accuse Israel of seeking to plunge the territory into chaos.

headline: PSG knock out Bayern to lock Champions League final with Arsenal
description: With a 1-1 draw, Paris Saint-Germain sealed a 6-5 aggregate victory at Bayern Munich on Wednesday.

headline: Moscow's Bauman University, the clandestine school training Russian hackers
description: According to 2,000 documents obtained by a consortium of international media, including Le Monde, the prestigious Bauman Moscow State Technical University has been discreetly training Russian military intelligence officers, particularly hackers.

Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

headline: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
description: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

headline: Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.
